chunks.en: Today I want to talk about asking a coworker for help.
chunks.ja: 今日は同僚に助けを求めることについて話したいです。

chunks.en: When I need help, I go and ask someone near me.
chunks.ja: 助けが必要なとき、私は近くの人のところへ聞きに行きます。

chunks.en: I usually ask a person who knows the work well.
chunks.ja: たいてい、仕事をよく知っている人に頼みます。

chunks.en: I try to say what I need in a short way.
chunks.ja: 必要なことを短く伝えるようにしています。

chunks.en: For example, I say my problem and the error clearly.
chunks.ja: 例えば、自分の問題とエラーをはっきり言います。

chunks.en: This way, my coworker can help me very fast.
chunks.ja: こうすると、同僚がとても早く助けてくれます。

chunks.en: After they help me, I always say thank you.
chunks.ja: 助けてもらったら、私はいつもお礼を言います。

chunks.en: So asking for help makes my work much easier.
chunks.ja: だから、助けを求めることは仕事をずっと楽にしてくれます。

outline: Opening: talking about asking a coworker for help
Point 1: go to someone near me who knows the work
Point 2: say my need in a short, clear way
Wrap-up: always say thank you and work gets easier

modelTalk.text: Sometimes I need help at work. When I'm stuck, I go and ask someone near me. I don't wait too long. I usually ask a person who knows the work well. That person can help me fast. First, I think about my problem. Then I try to say what I need. I keep it short and clear. I don't talk too much. I just ask one simple question. My coworker listens and then shows me. It's easy when I ask the right way. After they help me, I always say thank you. I think that's important. A small thank you feels good. So I try to be kind too.

chunks.en: Today I want to talk about asking a coworker for help.
chunks.ja: 今日は同僚に助けを求めることについて話したいです。

chunks.en: When I need help, I go and ask someone near me.
chunks.ja: 困ったとき、私は近くの人に聞きに行きます。

chunks.en: I usually ask a person who knows the work well.
chunks.ja: たいてい、仕事をよく知っている人に頼みます。

chunks.en: I try to say what I need in a short way.
chunks.ja: 必要なことを短く伝えるようにしています。

chunks.en: For example, I say I have a small server problem.
chunks.ja: 例えば、小さなサーバーの問題があると言います。

chunks.en: After they help me, I always say thank you.
chunks.ja: 助けてもらったら、私はいつもお礼を言います。

chunks.en: So asking for help makes my work much easier.
chunks.ja: だから助けを求めることで、私の仕事はずっと楽になります。

outline: Opening: asking a coworker for help
Point 1: who I ask and how I ask
Point 2: give a short, clear example and say thanks

modelTalk.text: Sometimes work is hard for me. When I need help, I don't just sit there. I get up and I go to someone near me. I usually ask a person who knows the work well. That's the fastest way for me. I try to stay calm. I say what I need in a short, clear way. I don't talk too much. I just tell them the problem. Most people are kind, and they help me. After they help me, I always say thank you. It's a small thing, but it matters a lot. Next time, maybe I can help them too. We work better together.
